channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russia claims it captured another town in eastern Ukraine but Kyiv's troops say the fight continues
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian troops in eastern Ukraine have captured the stronghold of Kurakhove after a monthslong battle, Russia’s Defense Ministry claimed Monday, adding the wrecked town to the Kremlin’s list of conquests during the almost three-year war.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's Trudeau is expected to announce his political future after facing growing calls to resign
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Facing growing calls to resign, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is expected to make an announcement about his political future this week.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated Maduro to meet Biden at White House
item.description: A senior U.S. administration official has told the Associated Press that the Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election will meet with U.S. President Joe Biden at the White House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Austrian far right gets mandate to try to lead a government for the first time since World War II
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The leader of Austria's Freedom Party received a mandate Monday to form a new government, which would be the first headed by the far right since World War II if he succeeds.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK leader Starmer slams 'lies and misinformation' after attacks from Elon Musk
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has condemned “lies and misinformation” that he says are undermining U.K. democracy.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea carries out first missile launch test since Trump's election
item.description: North Korea launched its first missile test since Election Day on Monday, strongly implying that it does not plan to play nice with President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German Christmas market attack victim dies at hospital weeks later, increasing death toll to 6
item.description: A woman who was injured during the Dec. 20 attack at a German Christmas market died at a hospital on Monday, increasing the death toll to six.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the siege outside South Korea's presidential compound
item.description: A standoff between rival government forces outside the presidential compound in South Korea is a startling development, even for observers used to the country’s famously rough and tumble politics.
item.dc:creator: Foster Klug, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea says North Korea fired a ballistic missile into the sea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Monday fired a ballistic missile that flew 1,100 kilometers (685 miles) before landing in waters between the Korean Peninsula and Japan, South Korea’s military said, extending its weapons testing weeks before Donald Trump returns as U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands protest in Montenegro to demand ouster of top security officials over mass shooting
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Several thousand people rallied in Montenegro on Sunday demanding the resignations of top security officials over the shooting earlier this week that left 12 people dead, including two children.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean anti-corruption agency and police debate tougher measures to detain impeached president
item.description: South Korea’s anti-corruption agency and police have been debating more forceful measures to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after their previous attempt was blocked by the presidential security service last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader urges supporters to demonstrate to force Maduro to leave office
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado on Sunday urged supporters to demonstrate across the country, saying in a video message that President Nicolás Maduro will not step down on his own and they “must make him leave.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy's Meloni makes surprise Mar-a-Lago visit to meet with Trump
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni made a surprise visit to Mar-a-Lago to meet with President-elect Trump on Saturday. They were joined by a number of Trump’s expected nominees for his forthcoming administration, according to a pool report, including Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: China’s decision this week to slap several major U.S. defense firms with penalizing trade measures is being viewed as a “shot across the bow” ahead of President-elect Trump’s inauguration. The move — targeting defense contractors Raytheon, Boeing, General Dynamics and Lockheed Martin — adds to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: South Korean protesters brave cold to demand Yoon's ouster as detention deadline looms
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Hundreds of South Koreans, bundled up against freezing temperatures and snow, rallied overnight into Sunday near the residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol, calling for his ouster and arrest, as authorities prepared to renew their efforts to detain him over his shor
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Costas Simitis, former Greek prime minister and socialist leader, dies at 88
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Costas Simitis, former prime minister of Greece and the architect of the country’s joining the common European currency, the euro, has died at age 88, state TV ERT reported.
item.dc:creator: DEMETRIS NELLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza kill at least 21, hospital workers say, as talks underway
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 21 people, including at least two children, on Saturday in Gaza, hospital staff said, while a new effort at ceasefire talks was underway in Qatar after nearly 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Central American troops arrive in Haiti to reinforce mission to restore order
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — About 150 military police officers from Central America have arrived in Haiti to reinforce the embattled government's fight against violent gangs that have upended daily life for millions in the Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German leader is more worried about Musk's backing of a far-right party than his insults
item.description: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz says he's staying “cool” against critical personal comments made by Elon Musk but finds it worrying that Musk makes the effort to get involved in Germany's general election by endorsing the far-right Alternative for Germany party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump announces Middle East appointment: Hopefully she's learned her lesson
item.description: President-elect Trump tapped Morgan Ortagus, a former State Department spokesperson, to serve as deputy special presidential envoy for Middle East peace in his next administration. Trump noted in his announcement that Ortagus previously was critical of him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: 1 cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, The Hill

item.title: Jake Sullivan, Biden discussed possibility of hitting Iran nuclear program: report
item.description: President Biden was presented scenario and response options on how to handle Iran should it make advances in developing a nuclear weapon, though the president has not signed off on hitting Tehran's program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Iran executes over 1K prisoners in 2024, highest total in 30 years, report says
item.description: A new report claimed Iran executed an unprecedented number of people in 2024. Most of the executions took place following the election of its new president in late July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, WGN 9

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: World population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, WFLA, NBC
